senses.definition: the act of hammering again or more forcefully on something, typically to repair, shape, or secure it further
examples: The rehammering of the metal sheet reshaped its edges.
After a misfit, the rehammering process was required to fit the joints.

senses.definition: third-person singular of rehammer, meaning to hammer again or more strongly
examples: He rehammers the rivet to ensure a tight seal.
They rehammers the metal piece after initial shaping.

senses.definition: to assign a new or additional handicap to someone or something, especially in a game or competition
examples: The officials decided to rehandicap the players after the first round.
The policy aims to rehandicap competitors to level the field.

senses.definition: to handle something again or in a different way
examples: We had to rehandle the package when it arrived damaged.
They rehandle the route to avoid traffic.

senses.definition: past tense of rehandle; handled again or in a different way
examples: The agent rehandled the claim after new evidence appeared.
The order was rehandled to correct the address.

senses.definition: a person or mechanism that rehandles something; in computing, a component that processes or redirects events or data again
examples: The rehandler in the system manages retries for failed messages.
A human rehandler adjusted the customer’s request.

senses.definition: third-person singular of rehandle; handles something again
examples: She rehandles the package to ensure it is safe.
The assistant rehandles the customer inquiry.

senses.definition: the act of handling something again or differently
examples: Rehandling the documents revealed an error in the file.
Rehandling is required when the product is defective.

senses.definition: plural of rehandling; instances of handling again
examples: The rehandlings of the goods caused some delays.
Several rehandlings were necessary to secure the shipment.

senses.definition: to hang something again or in a different place
examples: The painting was rehung after the renovation.
They rehung the coat rack in a more convenient spot.

senses.definition: past tense of rehang; hung again or in a different position
examples: The frame was rehanged to balance the wall.
They rehanged the curtain rods after repainting.

senses.definition: the act of hanging something again or anew
examples: The rehanging of the artwork took place after the floor was cleaned.

senses.definition: third-person singular of rehang; hangs again or in a different place
examples: They rehangs the picture to improve balance on the wall.

senses.definition: to happen again or to occur again; colloquially, to occur again in another way
examples: If the issue persists, we may rehappen the same discussion tomorrow.
We might rehappen the test under different conditions.

senses.definition: to harden again or become harder after softening
examples: The clay began to rehardening as it dried in the sun.
Temperature changes can reharden the material.

senses.definition: past tense of rehardern; became hard again
examples: The surface rehardened after cooling.
The coating rehardened once dry.

senses.definition: the process of hardening again
examples: The rehardening of the cement improves durability.

senses.definition: third-person singular of rehardens; hardens again
examples: The concrete reharden s over time with moisture.
This treatment reharden s the surface.

senses.definition: to re-apply harm or to cause harm again; usually in metaphorical sense about harm in relationships or policy
examples: The policy could reharm vulnerable groups if enacted.
He warned that the plan might reharm the economy.

senses.definition: the process of restoring or creating new harmony in music by adjusting chords and tones
examples: The composer explored reharmonization to give the piece a modern feel.
Reharmonization can alter the mood without changing the melody.

senses.definition: to alter the harmony of a musical work by changing chords or progressions
examples: The arranger chose to reharmonize the standard tune for a more contemporary sound.
reharmonize the bridge to create tension.

senses.definition: past tense of reharmonize; altered the harmony of a musical work
examples: The piece was reharmonized to suit a jazz ensemble.
They reharmonized the chorus for greater color.

senses.definition: third-person singular of reharmonize; alters the harmony of a piece
examples: The arranger reharmonizes the bridge to add tension.
She often reharmonizes standards for performances.

senses.definition: the act of applying new harmony to a musical work
examples: Reharmonizing can give an old song a fresh mood.

senses.definition: to put a harness on again or to fit with a harness again; used metaphorically to regain control or energy
examples: The team sought to rehar ness the project after the setback.
Efforts were made to rehar ness resources for the campaign.

senses.definition: alternate spelling for rehar ness; see above
